body:
## Summary

In the **desktop (Electron) app**, web search should work **out of the box** — even with *agent permission OFF* — and with a **wider** reach than the browser demo, because the desktop shell ships the [`web-capture`](https://github.com/) dependency and can spawn a **local Playwright/Puppeteer** headless browser in the background. Today it does neither: it returns the **same DuckDuckGo-Instant-Answer + Wikipedia/Wikidata reciprocal-rank-fusion** result the browser demo produces, and it **never touches Google, Bing, or any real search engine**, nor does it launch a local browser.

Verified on **v0.297.1**. Screenshot: desktop header reads `Desktop - in-process - agent permission off`, query "Search for Elon Musk" returns the identical `top 10 after reciprocal rank fusion (k = 60)` / `via duckduckgo#1 / via wikipedia#1` output as the web demo.

## Evidence

`formal-ai environments` — the desktop environment advertises richer tools than the browser:

```
desktop tools: desktop_shell, formal-ai serve, v1_chat_completions, v1_graph,
  export_memory, import_memory, agent_permission_gate, tool_router,
  http_fetch, read_local_file, local_shell, code_exec_box_dind, memory_sync
```

But note what is **missing**: no `web_search` and **no web-capture / Playwright / Puppeteer** entry. So the wider capabilities are not wired into the desktop environment's tool set.

Searching the installed binary confirms there is **no headless-browser runtime** and **no non-DDG search engine**:

- Playwright appears only as a **code-generation topic** in seed meanings ("I can write a Playwright script", `data/seed/meanings-playwright.lino`) — not as a runtime capability.
- One string even admits the gap: `"static Google AI Mode capture did not include a replayable transcript; use browser-backed web-capture support"` — i.e. `web-capture` is the intended mechanism, but it is not exercised.
- Search-engine endpoints in the binary: **DuckDuckGo only**. No `google.com/search`, no `bing.com`, no SerpAPI, no engine plurality.

## Expected

Environment-aware tool calling: the desktop app should detect that it can do **more** than a sandboxed browser and use it automatically —

1. **Out of the box, agent-permission-off:** real web search across multiple engines (Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, …), not just the CORS-safe DDG Instant Answer endpoint. Read-only search + fetch shouldn't require agent permission; that gate is for filesystem/shell side effects.
2. **Use the shipped `web-capture` dependency** to spawn a local Playwright/Puppeteer headless browser in the background for pages that block direct fetch / need JS rendering / need real SERP scraping.
3. Wire `web_search` (+ the browser-backed capture path) into the `desktop` environment's tool set so it is actually reachable.

## Why this matters

The browser demo is the **minimum** fallback (CORS-only, keyless DDG). The desktop app has strictly more power and should never silently fall back to the demo path. This is part of the same environment-aware tool-calling gap as the agentic-mode issue (filed separately) but is distinct: it affects desktop **even without agentic mode / API involvement**.


---
## Acceptance (all engines, all languages, out of the box)
Desktop web search must work for **10-20+ NL phrasings per language** (all supported/added languages, #745/#706), query real engines (Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, …) and use the local Playwright/Puppeteer `web-capture` path — with **agent permission OFF**, no extra setup. Same environment-aware principle as #746; shares memory per #756.


---
## Scope widening: desktop tool parity (internal + external), specialized-first selection

The desktop app should expose the **same common agentic tools as external tools** that the best CLIs/TUIs ship — implemented **out of the box** — while read-only tools double as **internal tools for thinking** (some already exist in the browser env: `concept_lookup, fact_lookup, wikipedia_lookup, calculator, read_local_file, web_search`).

Current desktop env tools are limited: `desktop_shell, formal-ai serve, v1_chat_completions, v1_graph, export/import_memory, agent_permission_gate, tool_router, http_fetch, read_local_file, local_shell, code_exec_box_dind, memory_sync` — **missing** the CLI-common set (grep, glob, list_directory, edit, write, web_search, web_fetch, todo/plan, subagent/task — see #758).

**Requested:**
1. **External agentic tools out of the box in desktop agentic mode**, matching the best CLI tools — the shared capability set from #758: read, write, edit, multi-edit, shell/bash, grep, glob, list_directory, read_many_files, web_search, web_fetch, todo/plan, subagent/task.
2. **Internal (read-only) tools for thinking** — reuse read-only tools (read_file, grep, glob, list_directory, web_search/fetch, calculator, lookups) as internal reasoning steps in the Universal Problem-Solving Algorithm, not just as user-visible actions.
3. **Specialized-first selection strategy:** when the environment provides a specialized tool, use it (best control + native rendering + UX); when a request is out of scope of the available specialized tools, fall back to **bash as the universal tool**. Most tools should be usable so the agent has fine-grained control across environments.
4. **Route by capability, not name** (per #758) so the same intent maps to whatever the environment/harness names it.

This keeps behavior consistent whether the desktop runs in-process (out-of-box tools) or via a passthrough CLI (see the desktop passthrough issue), and shares the single memory store (#756). Also applies to the VS Code extension env.


---
## Implementation spec (desktop web search)
- Add real search-engine providers behind `web_search` in the desktop/electron env: Google (Programmable Search / SERP), Bing (Web Search API or scrape), plus the existing DuckDuckGo — with a provider-priority list and reciprocal-rank fusion already used in the browser demo.
- Use the shipped **web-capture** dependency to spawn a local Playwright/Puppeteer (headless Chromium) for JS-rendered pages, CORS-blocked fetches, and SERP scraping; wire it into `web_fetch`/`web_search` for the desktop env.
- Gate: read-only web search/fetch works with **agent permission OFF**; the permission gate applies only to fs/shell writes.
- Add `web_search` + the browser-capture capability to the `desktop` env tool set (and the external-tools set for agentic mode).
- **Fixture:** desktop "Search for X" returns results sourced from >1 engine incl. Google/Bing, and a JS-heavy page fetch succeeds via headless browser, with agent permission off.

comments.body: _Folded into the issue description_ (widened): the all-natural-languages parity (en/ru/hi/zh + any added via #706) and all-tools coverage (codex, agent, gemini, qwen, opencode, grok, cursor — claude excluded from the required sweep, may be spot-checked via `-p`) acceptance criteria now live in the description above, with the 10–20+ NL-variations-per-intent generalization mandate tracked in #745. Verify e2e via the real TUIs — #671.

## 🚨 Solution Draft Failed

The automated solver stopped before creating a pull request, so no PR was opened for this issue.

### Failure
- **Repository**: `link-assistant/formal-ai`
- **Issue**: #747
- **Tool**: `agent`
- **Requested model**: `formal-ai`

**Reason**
```text
Invalid model name
```

### What you can do
- Resolve the repository, account, permissions, or environment problem described above, then rerun the solver.
- Repository owner or Hive Mind administrator path: handle manual recreation or fix of the repository when the required action is outside the requester access.

Logs were not attached because `--attach-logs` was not enabled.

title: Desktop app: web search degraded to browser-demo DDG path — no Google/Bing, no web-capture/Playwright headless browser (should work out-of-the-box, even with agent permission off) (v0.297.1)
